result:
**Goal: Add a Morphogenesis Mode — simulate biological embryonic development from a single cell.**

A single cell divides, differentiates, and self-organizes into a complex multicellular organism using morphogen gradients, gene regulatory networks, and local cell-cell signaling. Each cell carries a genome controlling division rules, differentiation responses, and morphogen production. Watch a fertilized "egg" develop into organized tissues with distinct cell types, boundaries, and emergent body plans — all in ASCII.

This builds directly on the project's strongest themes (self-modifying rules, reaction-diffusion, chemotaxis, evolution) while filling a gap: no existing mode simulates the *developmental biology* process of morphogenesis — arguably the most visually dramatic example of emergence in nature. Starting from ONE cell and watching structured complexity unfold is a uniquely compelling narrative that none of the 116 current modes deliver.
